title: Tender Zhejiang Squid Salt-and-Pepper-Squid
description: A yeasty, shattering Zhejiang-style squid dish featuring beef stock and a double-fried twist.
instructions: Dredge the squid pieces in a mixture of flour, cornstarch, and baking soda.
Dip the floured squid in the beaten egg whites, coating evenly.
Double-fry the squid in hot oil at 180°C for 2 minutes, then set aside.
Increase oil temperature to 220°C and re-fry the squid until puffed and golden.
Drain the squid on paper towels and sprinkle with salt and black pepper.
Return the squid to the hot oil and quickly toss to coat with a small amount of oil.
Transfer the squid to a serving platter and garnish with chopped scallions.
Serve immediately, accompanied by a side of steamed vegetables or rice.
For an added layer of flavor, drizzle with a small amount of beef stock and oil before serving.
Strain the oil from the fried squid and discard.
Season with additional salt and black pepper to taste, if needed.
